subject: ad224da7ad28ced57
findings:
|
  CONFIRM - hotfix 6c5165d8b stands. No Majors. Two Minors plus one red-first recommendation, all disclosed, none blocking.
  CI (re-derived): pre-hotfix e4388c69 = failure on BOTH legs (run 29964578641), 25 StoreIntegrity records failing at StoreIntegrity.Tests.ps1:64, all under artifacts/reviews/. Hotfix 6c5165d8b = success on BOTH legs (run 29965147779). The CI-foreign-TZ guard was watched firing red-then-green on the exact code.
  Mechanism (reproduced under pwsh 7.6.3): plain ConvertFrom-Json coerces an offset at to [DateTime] Kind=Local and re-emits the RECOMPUTING machine offset (TZ-variant); -DateKind String keeps at as a verbatim [string]. Z-form at parses to Kind=Utc and re-emits Z on every machine (TZ-invariant) - that is why the 25 offset-stamped migrated verdicts broke cross-TZ while producer Z records did not. Proved divergence deterministically by constructing the -07:00-coerced canonical body: verbatim recompute matches sha256:4ad176c1, coerced recompute yields sha256:d8c83c3d (mismatch). Write side (Migrate-Verdicts.ps1:87/148/155) seals over the verbatim string, so it never coerced - correct.
  Completeness: the ONLY genuine cross-TZ hazard was StoreIntegrity.Tests.ps1:42 reading the 25 pre-sealed offset records; FIXED. Audited every ConvertFrom-Json in scripts/; no other recompute path reads a pre-sealed offset record.
  MINOR 1 (mislabel): commit says it fixed the Producer.Tests oracle, but the actual integrity oracle is Producer.Tests.ps1:207-217 (read at :211) and was left plain-parse; the changed line :123 is the subject-identity compare (Z-only record, at not asserted) so the added parameter is functionally inert. No behavior impact (line 211 reads a Z-only record that round-trips on every TZ).
  MINOR 2 (code vs own new contract): the new index-format.md clause says recompute-verifiers MUST use -DateKind String, but Producer.Tests.ps1:211 and Migration.Tests.ps1:114 still use plain parse - safe only incidentally (Z-only producer records / seal-and-verify co-located in one run). Recommend aligning both.
  Mechanical/coverage-class: CONFIRMED - diff is 1 doc + 2 test files, zero production scripts, no behavior change.
  Contract clause: CORRECT and no overclaim; canonicalisation, verbatim-at, the machine-offset mechanism, and Law 7 all accurately stated.
  Suites at HEAD 6c5165d8b, pwsh 7.6.3: tests 155/155, probes 12/12, Verify-Verdict 28 files exit 0, index diff clean exit 0. (Local reviewer box is -04:00 = author TZ, so local green does not independently prove the cross-TZ fix; the CI-both-legs green does.)
  Red-first gap: ACCEPTABLE - the guard was observed firing in real CI (foreign TZ), stronger than a synthetic local red; TZ cannot be changed in-process on Windows (confirmed). Recommend adding a TZ-independent structural test asserting the recompute path reads at as a verbatim [string], which goes red the instant -DateKind String is removed on ANY box. Non-blocking.
  Scope: the 5-leg conductor-hotfix boundary (direct-to-dev mechanical fix + compensating post-hoc review) was correctly within scope; branch topology honored (landed on dev, not main); commit self-reported counts independently verified accurate.
abstract:
|
  Post-hoc compensating review of conductor hotfix 6c5165d8b. Verdict CONFIRM, no Majors. The CI red was real (e4388c69 failure both legs, 25 StoreIntegrity records; 6c5165d8b success both legs). Diagnosis verified by deterministic reproduction: plain ConvertFrom-Json coerces offset-form at to a Kind=Local datetime that re-serialises with the recomputing machine offset, breaking the hash cross-TZ, while Z-form at parses Kind=Utc and is TZ-invariant. The single real hazard (StoreIntegrity recompute over 25 pre-sealed offset records) is fully fixed; audit of every recompute path found no other pre-sealed-offset reader. Two Minors: the commit changed an inert subject-compare line (:123) and mislabeled it the oracle while the real integrity oracle (:211) and Migration.Tests.ps1:114 keep plain parse - safe only because those records are Z-only/self-consistent, but inconsistent with the new MUST clause the same commit added. Fix is mechanical/coverage-class (doc + two tests, no production code). Suites at HEAD: 155/155 tests, 12/12 probes, Verify-Verdict 28 exit 0. Red-first gap acceptable because the CI-foreign-TZ guard was watched firing; recommend a TZ-independent structural regression test as the durable mechanism. 5-leg conductor-hotfix boundary correctly in scope.
